text: Debugging is a crucial skill for programmers. When your code doesn't work, start by identifying the bug. Read error messages carefully—they often point to the problem. Then, use a systematic approach: isolate the issue by testing small parts of your code. Debugging tools like breakpoints allow you to pause execution and inspect variables. Another powerful strategy is logging. Add print statements or use a logging library to track what your program is doing at each step. This helps you see unexpected values or flow. Remember, patience is key. By following these steps and staying organized, you can solve bugs more effectively and improve your code quality over time.
